observations.recommendation:
1.15 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary correcting
entries in the Cash in Local Treasury Account in the JEV and reflect
such adjustments to the affected Financial Statements;
b. direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the RCD within 15 days
following each reporting month to the Municipal Accountant; and
c. implement frequent reconciliation of records between the accounting
and the treasurer’s office.

2.22 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require:
a. the Municipal Assessor to submit up-to-date Assessment Roll to the Acting
Municipal Treasurer and the former to immediately update the Real
Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card;
b. the Acting Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant of a
duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible for the year as the basis for setting-up the RPT and SET
Receivables; and
c. the Municipal Accountant to ensure that (i) the correct basis is used to book
up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year, (ii) properly
record the transactions pertaining to the collections of property taxes, and
(iii) analyze the entries provided in order to rectify the noted variances of
the affected accounts.

3.19 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to:
i. prepare JEV to:
1.1 re-classify the completed projects to its proper PPE
account;
1.2 to correct the misclassification of PPE to another
account;
1.3 to correct the erroneous recognition and non-recognition
of PPE account;
2. ensure that depreciation is properly provided for each class of PPE as
discussed in PAG3 of the IPSAS 17 and provide for the detailed
schedule of depreciation;
3. disclose in the Notes to FS the correct amounts to support the data
presented the Financial Statements;
4. prepare and maintain property ledger cards and subsidiary ledgers of each
PPE account;
b. require the General Service Officer to prepare and maintain property
cards for all PPEs of the Municipality and effect regular reconciliation
with the ledger cards of the Accounting Office; and
c. require the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the approved Physical
Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the
scheduled start of inventory- taking activities, conduct and complete the
physical inventory in three months or less and ensure full implementation of
“One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in COA Circular No.
2020-006.

4.15 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the personnel concerned the
immediate payment of the Municipality’s due obligations and moving
forward, ensure timely and prompt payment in order to maintain good
standing with the bank.

4.16 We also recommended to the Municipal Accountant to draw necessary
adjusting journal entries to rectify the deficiencies noted and provide sufficient
disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements.

5.11 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant
to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers/Records for the balance of the
Trust Liabilities and provide sufficient and necessary disclosures in the Notes
to Financial Statements.

6.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct:
a. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to initiate the
reconciliation of records covering the periods CY 2018 to CY 2021 and
should arrive at the correct balance of the unspent portion of the DRRM
Fund and moving forward, to implement regular reconciliation
particularly at year end;
b. the Municipal Accountant to:
i. Draw an adjusting journal entry to record the correct balance to
the Trust Liabilities- Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund account; and
ii. Maintain and update the related subsidiary ledgers.

7.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant
to create and maintain a special account, complete with the special account subsidiary
ledger, trial balance and financial statements for the operation of municipality’s
local economic enterprise.

8.17 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a. Require the contractor, through the BAC, the immediate submission of the
identified lacking documents in support to the contract agreement, and for
future implementation, ensure the completeness of supporting documents
prior to the perfection of the contract agreement,
b. Fast track the implementation of the projects and ensure its completeness
within the target period; and
c. Submit accurate and correct report to oversight agencies for proper
monitoring.

9.12 We recommended and the LCE ensure that Acknowledgment Receipt be
issued for the receipt of In-Kind Donations and require the following:
a. the General Services Officer- Designate, and the Municipal Health Officer
to coordinate in the preparation of the monthly reports on the receipt and
distribution/utilization/issuance of donated items and account for the
unutilized items at year end, if any and prepare and maintain a Registry
of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.
b. the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary disclosure in the Notes to
FS as to the quantity of received, issued and balance of all donation-in-kind
received by the Municipality, with categorization as to food items,
medicines and medical supplies, hygiene kits, and others.

10.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Acting Municipal
Treasurer to immediately send collection letters to the delinquent property owners and provide
a copy of their payments to this Office, and prepare the Notice of Delinquency in the Payment of the
Real Property Tax and post the same at the main entrance of the municipal hall and in a publicly
accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay of the local government unit concerned; and

10.10 We likewise recommended that the legislative officials, in consultation with the
Acting Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to draw up plans to
strategize and strengthen the collection drive beyond the required posting and
sending of notices.

11.15 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct:
a. the LDRRM Officer and the MPDC to meticulously and judiciously plan
the available fund maintained in the special trust fund and include in the
budget process;
b. the Municipal Accountant to prepare the revised LDRRMF Utilization
Report incorporating the observations noted by the audit team, and submit
the same to the oversight agencies; moving forward, ensure timely
submission of the report within the prescribed period; and
c. the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate registries of RAAO for
the QRF, MOOE, and Capital Outlay and closely monitor the charges
made to the special trust fund.

1.12 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require:
a. the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to investigate the
unreconciled balances between the Cashbook records and the GL and to
draw necessary adjusting entries to correct the deficiencies.
b. all revenue collectors to turn over the full amount of their collections at
the close of business day to the Municipal Treasurer and the latter be
required to deposit intact all collections to the depository banks of the
Municipality.

1.13 The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer were amenable on the
deficiencies noted and will exert efforts in order to investigate the unreconciled
balances.

1.14 As to the stale check from PCSO, the Municipal Treasurer explained that upon
receipt of the check, it was already stale and the RCC inadvertently acknowledged
the check because such RCC was not aware and still new to the office. The
Municipal Treasurer promised to strictly monitor and supervise the RCCs so that
mandatory remittance will be followed.

2.13 We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately prepare and submit
the BRS of all its depository bank accounts, take up reconciling items, and
prepare necessary adjusting journal entries; (ii) regularly reconcile the
book balances and bank records vis-a-vis the cashbook and prepare
adjusting entries, if any; (iii) regularly submit the mandatory reports and
the supporting documents within the reglementary period;
b) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to: (i) post the balances of the Cash in
Bank- LCCA in the cashbook, with proper segregation of the sources of
funds; (ii) coordinate with the depository bank to ensure the prompt
delivery of the monthly bank statements or its equivalent to facilitate the
timely reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts; and
c) Provide accounting proficient support staff in the Accounting Unit to
ensure that the mandatory reports are submitted within the reglementary
period.

3.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the:
1. The Municipal Assessor and the MPDC to act on the processing of the
TCTs of the parcels of land reported under the name of the Municipality,
and to register them with the Register of Deeds without any further
delay;
2. Municipal Accountant:
a. to trace the period or date of acquisition of the assets and compute
the depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets as
provided under COA Circular 2003-007 and prepare the necessary
adjusting journal entries to update the carrying value of the said
assets;
b. to prepare Journal Entry Voucher on the adjustment for
depreciation, correction of erroneous entries and the proper
classification of accounts in the financial statements; and
c. provide subsidiary ledgers for each class of PPE.

4.13 We recommended and Management agreed, that the LCE require:
a. The Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Local Disaster and Risk
Reduction and Management Officer, and the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator to ensure that all PPAs chargeable against the
5% LDRRMF must conform with the Appropriation Ordinance and that
all funds available for use shall be accompanied with well-planned PPAs;
and
b. The Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entries to
correct the errors and ensure that the amount and details of the
unexpended balance of LDRRMF shall be discussed in the Notes to
Financial Statements.

5.5 We recommended and Management agreed, that the LCE instruct the
Municipal Accountant to reconcile the actual amounts presented in the
SCBAA and the actual amounts presented in the financial statements and
disclose the said reconciliation in the notes to financial statements, to enable
the Municipality to better discharge its accountability obligations.

6.8 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require all revenue collectors to
turn over the full amount of their collections at the close of business day to the
Municipal Treasurer and the latter be required to deposit intact all collections
to the depository banks of the Municipality.

6.9 We likewise recommended that the LCE to take urgent action pertaining to
the RCC not performing his expected duties and responsibilities.

7.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to capacitate and mobilize its
Inventory Committee, cause the conduct of a complete physical count of all
properties and unissued inventories of the Municipality, the subsequent
preparation and submission of the RPCPPE and RPCI, and make way for the
availment of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances, pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2020-006 and the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

8.11 We recommended and Management agreed, that the LCE require the
submission of complete supporting documents for the grant of COVID-19
SRA.

8.12 This recommendation is without prejudice to the possible issuance of Notice of
Suspension if the Municipality fails to submit the afore-mentioned required
documents.

9.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the submission of complete
supporting documents for the grant of COVID-19 AHDP.

9.7 This recommendation is without prejudice to the possible issuance of Notice of
Suspension if the Municipality fails to submit the afore-mentioned required
documents.

10.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to approve only payments for
infrastructure projects that are duly supported with complete documents,
pursuant to the relevant provisions of PD 1445 and the 2016 RIRR of RA No.
9184.

10.8 This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension or Disallowances, if warranted.

11.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Strictly comply with Section 49 of GAA FY 2021 which mandates that the
net take home pay should not be lower than ₱5,000.00. If in any case, the
personal loan of an employee will cause its net take home pay to be lower
than what is mandated by law, said loan should not be allowed to be
deducted from his salaries and should be settled or paid separately and
not be included in the payroll; and
b. Properly monitor and assess the existing loan obligations of LGU
personnel which are authorized as deductions to salaries and initiate
control measures.

12.14 We recommended and the LCE agreed that:
a. Direct the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to: (i)
prepare the Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern Plan in order
to assess the required number of JO workers to be hired based on necessity
and financial capability of the municipality; (ii) prepare a job description
to delineate the specific responsibilities of the JOs; (iii) prepare a Job
Order contract of service or Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and
b. Moving forward, direct the Accountant to make the necessary deductions
for all leave of absences.

13.13 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require that payment to lecturers,
resource persons, coordinators and facilitators be based on the guidelines set
forth under DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-1 and must be substantiated with
complete supporting documents.

13.14 We also recommended that the Accounting Unit shall ensure that creditable
withholding tax is deducted from payments made and the same must be
remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period.

1.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE): a) direct the
Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the SLCs; b) require the
General Services/Supply Office to keep and maintain SCs; c) order the
physical count of inventories and to prepare and submit the RPCI; d) require
the Municipal Accountant to ensure that issuances/distributions of inventory
items recorded in the books are supported with RSMI prepared by the
General Services/Supply Office, complete with RIS and other related
documents; and e) direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from recording
as outright expenses the purchased/acquired inventories, but to record the
same to their appropriate inventories accounts.

2.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to strictly and efficiently observe compliance with the Chart of
Accounts under GAM for LGUs with respect to recording of the
Municipality’s transactions in the books.

4.8 We recommended that the LCE: a) require the submission of the lacking
mandatory documentary requirements of the suppliers, otherwise, justify the
agency’s purchase dealings despite the question of supplier’s eligibility; and
b) direct the posting of the NOA, contracts and purchase orders in any
conspicuous place in the locality and in the GPPB online portal for Emergency
Procurement under the Bayanihan Act.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension or Disallowance.

5.8 We recommended that the LCE: a) direct the Municipal Accountant to submit
the necessary supporting documents as above-mentioned; b) require the
Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and Punong Barangay
concerned to submit an explanation on the replacements, additions and non-
payment of beneficiaries; and c) order the posting of the list of beneficiaries
who already received the emergency subsidy in the Municipality’s official
website, social media accounts, and at conspicuous places of the locality.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension or Disallowance.

6.8 We recommended that the LCE direct the submission of relevant documents
as mentioned above to support the validity and propriety of the payment of
COVID-19 Hazard Pay and Special Risk Allowance.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension or Disallowance.

7.6 We recommended that the LCE: a) direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure
completeness or sufficiency of supporting documents; and b) require the
issuance of ICSs to end-users of small tangible items.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension or Disallowance.

8.7 We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC to strictly comply with the
requirement of submission of contracts within five days from execution under
COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, and of sending
invitation to observers pursuant to 2016 RIRR of RA 9184.

9.11 We recommended that the LCE approve only payments for infrastructure
projects that are duly supported with the necessary documents, pursuant to
the relevant provisions of PD No. 1445, 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184, and COA
Circular No. 2012-001.
9.12 This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension or Disallowance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal Accountant to perform the following: (1) a) duly prepare the monthly and quarterly pre-closing trial balances and timely submit the same later than the 20th day after the end of the month; and (2) b) prepare and maintain complete ledgers and journals and submit the monthly and quarterly trial balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly bank reconciliation statements, including the bank statements, and provide details of the reconciling items included therein. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensures accuracy of the bank reconciliation statements.

We recommended that revenue collectors be required to turn-over/remit and report all collections received for the entire day to the Municipal Treasurer at the close of each business day and for the latter to deposit all collections intact daily or not later than the next banking day. We likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the former municipal treasurer to settle his undeposited collections.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) require the Local Treasurer and Accountant to maintain property cards and property, plant and equipment ledger cards, and instigate the conduct of physical inventory of PPE; b) directs all personnel concerned to prepare and maintain the necessary records and reports of Road Networks; c) executes the process of donation on purchased assets given to schools and requires the Local Accountant to drop from the books the related cost thereof; and d) directs the Local Accountant to make necessary journal entries for the correction of prior year’s misstatements and proper recognition of depreciation applicable to past and current years.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive reclassify the balance of Other Payables account amounting to P1,547,555.70 to Loans Payable-Domestic account; b) make a separate classification of the current and non-current portion of the balance thereof, including the Loans Payable-Domestic account’s balance, in the statement of financial position; and c) provide for the complete disclosures of the loans in the Notes to Financial Statements. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to create and maintain a special account for the loan, complete with the subsidiary ledger, trial balance and financial statements.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of RA 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991 on appropriations prior to project implementation. We likewise recommended that policy and guidelines be installed concerning the determination of beneficiaries of sanitary toilets.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the minimum funding for LDRRMF as required under RA 10121 and with all the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. We likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to cause the complete transfer of unexpended balances of LDRRMF to the Trust Fund account and the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary entries thereof and adjust the balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRM account at year-end.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to fully comply with the preparation and submission of GAD accomplishment report as enunciated under the Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 16-01 and 13-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary correcting entries for the proper adjustment of CIB-LCCA, taking into account the book errors and the restoration of the value of stale checks to the agency’s CIB-LCCA, and restore the overstatement in the Cash-Local Treasury account. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to deposit all collections regularly and intact.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers/Records for the balances of the controlling account appearing in the General Ledger and provide for details of the non-moving/dormant accounts.

We recommended that Management refrain from utilizing portions of LDRRMF for unplanned and non-disaster-related programs and transfer the unexpended balance thereof to the separate Trust Fund account. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly observe the accounting and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF as enunciated under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require strict compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 on the proper documentation of payment of wages.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to strictly comply with the provisions set forth under RA 9184 and its IRR on the invitation of observers and posting requirement of Invitation to Bid, Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and the approved contract.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit the lacking 52 paid disbursement vouchers and 99 checks reported as cancelled to COA for audit and verification. Henceforth, strictly observe the timely submission of accounts as required by Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and avoid further repetition of the delay.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to file a request for write-off of the receivable balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Finance Committee to include in the Annual Budget an appropriation equivalent to 1% of IRA to finance Local Council for Protection of Children activities, cause the creation of LCPC and adopt a comprehensive plan and program for the welfare of the children.

We recommended that management submit legal basis for the grant of honoraria to some national government officials otherwise, they should stop the payment of the same to the subject personnel. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of DBM Compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1 dated March 23, 1998 and Section 5.7 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowance resulting from post-audit of transactions.

We recommended that the management strictly ensure that transactions involving fund transfers be supported by the required documents before such funds are released pursuant to the requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all concerned officials/employees to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials and employees strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004. These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance resulting from post-audit of transactions. We recommend that management prepare and submit an Annual Procurement Plan which would serve as a guide in its procurement activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all executive officials and employees of the municipality faithfully discharge their duties and functions as provided under Section 444.b.1.X of RA 7160. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents. This recommendation is without prejudice to the result of post-audit.

We recommended immediate submission of the 38 vouchers and disbursement vouchers totaling P1,035,996.66 otherwise this office will disallow the disbursements and impose sanctions provided under Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to submit transactions/reports on time as required under Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and avoid further repetition of the delay.
We recommended submission of the disbursement vouchers to avoid disallowance and that the Municipal Accountant avoid delays in submission in future periods.
We recommended timely submission of accounts and supporting documents as required by COA Circulars and PD 1445.
We recommended filing requests for write-off of dormant balances as per COA Circular guidance.
We recommended creation of LCPC, appropriation of necessary funds and preparation of comprehensive plans.
We recommended submission of legal basis for honoraria and strict adherence to DBM and COA guidelines; recommendations without prejudice to notices of suspension/disallowance.
We recommended that transactions involving transfers be supported by required documents prior to release per COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We recommended immediate liquidation of outstanding cash advances and strict adherence to PD 1445 and relevant COA Circulars; prepare annual procurement plan.
We recommended submission of outstanding vouchers and supporting documents and compliance with COA submission requirements.
We recommended preparation and maintenance of subsidiary ledgers/records for controlling account balances and provide details of dormant accounts appearing in the general ledger.
We recommended refraining from utilizing portions of LDRRMF for unplanned programs and transfer unexpended balances to a separate Trust Fund account; strictly observe COA Circular No. 2012-002.
We recommended strict compliance with documentation requirements for payment of wages and adherence to PD 1445 and COA Circulars.
We recommended strict compliance with RA 9184 and its IRR on invitation of observers and posting requirements.
We recommended submission of the missing vouchers and checks for audit and verification and to avoid recurrence.
We recommended filing for write-off of dormant receivable balances as per COA Circular 2016-005.
We recommended creation of LCPC, allocation of budget, and preparation of required plans for children welfare.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal
Accountant to perform the following: a) duly prepare the monthly and
quarterly pre-closing trial balances and timely submit the same not later
than the 20th day after the end of the month; and b) prepare and maintain
complete ledgers and journals and submit the same upon every submission of
trial balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and timely submit the monthly bank reconciliation
statements, including the bank statements, and provide details of the
reconciling items included therein. We likewise recommended that the
Municipal Accountant ensures accuracy of the bank reconciliation
statements.

We recommended that revenue collectors be required to turn-over/remit and
report all collections received for the entire day to the Municipal Treasurer
at the close of each business day and for the latter to deposit all collections
intact daily or not later than the next banking day. We likewise
recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the former municipal
treasurer to settle his accountability on undeposited collections.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) requires the Local Treasurer and Accountant to maintain property cards
and property, plant and equipment ledger cards, and instigate the conduct
of physical inventory of PPE;
b) directs all personnel concerned to prepare and maintain the necessary
records and reports of Road Networks;
c) executes the process of donation on purchased assets given to schools and
requires the Local Accountant to drop from the books the related cost
thereof; and
d) directs the Local Accountant to make necessary journal entries for the
correction of prior year’s misstatements and proper recognition of
depreciation applicable to past and current years.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal
Accountant to: a) reclassify the balance of Other Payables account
amounting to P1,547,555.70 to Loans Payable-Domestic account; b) make a
separate classification of the current and non-current portion of the balance
thereof, including the Loans Payable-Domestic account’s balance, in the
statement of financial position; and c) provide for the complete disclosures of
the loans in the Notes to Financial Statements. We likewise recommended
that the Municipal Accountant be required to create and maintain a special
account for the loan, complete with the subsidiary ledger, trial balance and
financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensures strict compliance
with the provisions of RA 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991 on
appropriations prior to project implementation. We likewise recommended
that policy and guidelines be installed concerning the determination of
beneficiaries of sanitary toilets.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the minimum
funding for LDRRMF as required under RA 10121 and with all the
accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-
002 dated September 12, 2012. We likewise recommended that the Local
Chief Executive directs the Municipal Treasurer to cause the complete
transfer of unexpended balances of LDRRMF to the Trust Fund account and
the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary entries thereof and adjust
the balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRM account at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor revises the composition of the
Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and spearheads in the
preparation and formulation of the solid waste management plan by the
MSWMB.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the Municipal
Accountant to create and maintain a special account for each of the LGU’s
economic enterprises in the form of market and cemetery operations,
complete with the special account subsidiary ledger, trial balance and
financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the GAD Focal
Person to fully comply with the preparation and submission of GAD
accomplishment report as enunciated under the Joint Memorandum
Circular Nos. 16-01 and 13-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary correcting entries for the proper adjustment of CIB-LCCA, taking into account the book errors and the restoration of the amount or value of stale checks to the agency’s CIB-LCCA, and Cash-Local Treasury account. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to deposit all collections regularly and intact.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to properly recognize depreciation on property, plant and equipment and ensure fair presentation of PPE balances in accordance with PPSAS No. 17 and to maintain property, plant and equipment ledger cards, and instruct the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer to maintain property cards. We likewise recommended the constitution of the inventory committee to conduct physical count of all PPE and render a report thereon, in accordance with Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and the Handbook on Property and Supply Management.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers/Records for the balances of the controlling account appearing in the General Ledger and provide for the details of the non-moving/dormant accounts.

We recommended that Management ensure compliance with the minimum required appropriation for the 20% DF and refrain from utilizing portion of the 20% DF for non-related programs/projects/activities. We likewise recommended the submission of report on the utilization of continuing appropriation of 20% DF and henceforth, strictly observe the provisions set forth under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that Management refrain from utilizing portions of LDRRMF for unplanned and non-disaster-related programs, projects and activities and transfer the unexpended balance thereof to the separate Trust Fund account. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the strict observance of the accounting and reporting guidelines for the LDRRMF as enunciated under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require strict compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 on the proper documentation of payment of wages.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to fully comply with the preparation and submission of GAD accomplishment report as enunciated under the Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 16-01 and 13-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to submit the perfected contracts and their supporting documents within five (5) days from execution thereof and to rectify, if possible, all deficiencies noted in the conduct of legal and auditorial review of contracts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to strictly comply with the provisions set forth under RA 9184 and its IRR on the invitation of observers and posting requirement of Invitation to Bid, Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and the approved contract.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit the lacking 52 paid disbursement vouchers and 99 checks reported as cancelled to COA for audit and verification.

We recommended that Revenue Collection Clerks be required to turn over the full amount of their collections at the close of business day to the Municipal Treasurer, and for the latter to deposit intact his collections, as well as collections turned over to him by the collectors/tellers, with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day, in accordance with Section 307 of RA 7160 and Section 26 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted the 38 disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the procuring office furnish the Auditor with the required copies of contract documents/purchase/letter/job orders together with supporting documents as well as notice of Deliveries of equipment, and their supporting documents and notice of deliveries of material procured items within the prescribed period for review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: submit immediately the original copies of cancelled checks. In future, submit financial reports and documents within the prescribed dates of submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to submit the accounts and financial reports and to strictly observe the timely submission of the same in accordance with Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39, PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive should ensure timely submission; this recommendation is without prejudice to the result of post-audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management strictly ensure that transactions involving fund transfers be supported by the required documents before such funds are released pursuant to the requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Notified the concerned officials. The MOA were not submitted to the audit team.
observations.reason: The MOA were not submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a periodic reconciliation and prepare the bank reconciliation statement for SEF and Trust Fund in compliance with the provisions of Section 74 of PD 1445 and Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to file a request for write-off of the receivable account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected in accordance to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that management provide a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP for the unexpended previous years’ balance of the LDRRMF and render a monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the fund in the prescribed format. Also, we recommended that the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF be transferred to a special trust fund account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the municipal accountant to prepare correcting entries for the proper classification of the transferred LGSF-ADM fund and to maintain subsidiary records of each of the three (3) projects.

We recommended that the Local School Board strictly comply with the provisions of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 so as to allocate the Special Education Fund among the priority expenditures to efficiently achieve the purpose for which the fund was created.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Finance Committee to include in the Annual Budget an appropriation equivalent to 1% of IRA to finance Local Council for the Protection of Children activities, cause the creation of LCPC and adopt a comprehensive plan and program for the welfare of the children.

We recommended that the management through the Municipal Mayor strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the Revised RA 9184 and its applicable IRR, especially on the prohibition on splitting of contracts in its procurement transactions. We also recommend that the General Services Office be required to consolidate such requirements and for the BAC to conduct a quarterly bidding thereof.

We further recommended to discontinue procurement through reimbursement basis unless extremely urgent and necessary and indicate specifications in the RFQ for catering services to obtain the most economical cost.

We recommended that management submit legal basis for the grant of honoraria to some national government officials and employees, otherwise, they should stop the payment of the same to the subject personnel. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of DBM compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1 dated March 23, 1998 and Section 5.7 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and DBM Budget Circular 2007-1 dated April 23, 2007.

We recommended that management strictly comply Section 16.1 of the IRR of RA No. 9433 and payment must be supported by proof of actual service rendered during the period being claimed and that the same be provided with a computation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly withhold income taxes from its honoraria payments in accordance with Section 2.79 of Revenue Regulation (RR) No. 2-98 as amended by Section 3 of RR 10-2008.

We recommended that 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized to help achieve desirable socio-economic and environmental outcomes as provided for in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 and to comply with the submission of contract documents for evaluation within five (5) working days from its execution pursuant to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 22, 2009.
observations.managementAction:
municipal mayor spearheaded the implementa-
tion of the 20% development fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to strictly observe COA Circulars Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002 on the reporting of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Finding 4, Part II of this report.
observations.reason: See audit observation no. 2; report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board include as topmost priority the improvement of educational facilities and other priority programs/projects/activities enumerated in Section 100 (c) of RA 7160 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 Series of 1998, and to maximize its implementation to ensure that the desired benefits are obtained by a greater number of intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board realigned some of the SEF funded activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management monitor the PS expenditures and observe strictly the 55% Personnel Services limitation in the preparation of the annual budget as required in Section 325 (a) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor being the Local Chief Executive should always be guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and supplemental budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements were completely complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit immediately the original copies of cancelled checks. In future submit financial reports and documents within the prescribed dates of submission.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the cancelled checks for verification of audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management formulate an action plan to implement the 2015 audit recommendations and submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the audit team; copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 96 of RA 10717.
observations.managementAction: Formulated action plan to implement the audit recommendations and submit the AAPSI form; copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to draw a journal entry JEV and voucher to correct the error in the recording of the receipt of funds from the National Government Agencies and the utilization thereof in books of the General Fund. Likewise, require him to disclose in the Notes to the Combined Financial Statements the complete information required under PPSAS in the Notes to the Combined Financial Statements, particularly the reconciliation between actual amounts in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and the Statement of Financial Performance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal accountant prepared a complete information required under PPSAS in the Notes to the Combined Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The municipal accountant failed to disclose information in the Statement of Financial Performance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management strictly comply with the guidelines of DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 with regard to the appropriation and utilization of the 20% development fund.
observations.managementAction: Management already followed the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop charging to LDRRMF expenditures that are not related to disaster risk management activities and ensure that the MDRRM Plan reconcile with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 to avoid unnecessary charges to the LDRRMF. Further, cause the immediate submission of the Certificate of Title for the purchase of the land.
observations.managementAction: Complied with the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management sought the secure approval of the DSWD Secretary for the modification in distribution of ESA from P30,000.00 to P10,000.00 per household and submit an explanation on the exclusion of at least 348 persons as ESA beneficiaries and submit SB and Barangay Resolution.
observations.managementAction: sought the approval of the Secretary for the modification in distribution of the ESA P30,000.00 to P10,000.00 per household and submitted an explanation on the exclusion of at least 348 persons as ESA beneficiaries; submitted SB and Barangay Resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Social Welfare Development Officer be required to formulate policies and guidelines on the grant of financial assistance to individuals in crises situation and submit the same to the Sanggunian for approval pursuant to Section 483(b) of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the SB Resolution and complied with the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly observe the minimum take home pay requirement of P3,000.00 for each employees pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 10651 so as not to affect the efficiency and morale of the employees.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed that all concerned employees to comply with the minimum take home pay requirement.
observations.reason: Some employees are still receiving less than the prescribed net pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that fees collected for Bids/BAC documents be refunded to the suppliers who participate in the procurement process, pursuant to the provisions of Section 3.0 of DBM Budget Circular 2007-3 issued on November 29, 2007, under Appendix 23 of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed that all concerned employees provided to refund the amount of P113,450.00.
observations.reason: Employees still not refunded the amount of P113,450.00 by the concerned employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials and employees to strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 and immediately unliquidated cash advance; prepare the schedules showing complete detailed information on the unliquidated cash advances of prior years and reconcile discrepancies.
observations.managementAction: Notified the concerned officials/employee to immediately unliquidated their outstanding cash advance.
observations.reason: Some of the concerned officials and employees did not submit their Liquidation Reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the provisions of Section 74 of PD 1445, Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-001 dated October 2, 1996 and Section 10, Chapter 1 of the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units, Vol. II and prepare subsidiary records for General Fund only.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Bank Accountant prepared the Bank Reconciliation Statements for General Fund only.
observations.reason: Subsidiary records were not prepared by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant submit the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents immediately and improve internal controls to avoid recurrence; timely submission of the accounts as required by regulations.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the disbursement vouchers. This is without prejudice to the result of post audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the 38 disbursement vouchers totaling P1,035,996.66, otherwise this office will disallow subject disbursements and impose sanctions provided under Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to submit transactions/reports on time as required under Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and avoid further repetition of the delay.
observations.managementAction: Submitted some of the disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: Some of the disbursement vouchers were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare an inventory list of all insurable properties for GSIS Fund coverage; conduct a physical count of all PPE and drop from the books those that are unserviceable; properties will be evaluated for the application of its insurance policy.
observations.managementAction: Motor vehicles were already insured at the General Fund administered by GSIS property insurance coverage.
observations.reason: Some buildings and other equipment already insured. Other equipment yet to be insured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Development Plan be carefully formulated to include the time frames for projects and programs to facilitate the monitoring of accomplishment. Likewise, require the responsible officials to ensure the preparation of program of works, plans and specifications and detailed estimates prior to project implementation. Further, copies of perfected contracts and its supporting documents should be submitted within five (5) days after approval.
observations.managementAction: Motor vehicles were insured. Submitted the contracts, program of works, and plans and specifications for some projects; advised to submit perfected contracts and supporting documents.
observations.reason: Some contracts and supporting documents were not submitted; prevented the conduct of inspection and validation of actual accomplishment.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to draw a journal entry and voucher to correct the error in the recording of the receipt of funds from the National Government Agencies and the utilization thereof, in books of the General Fund, Water System Project, and the Trust Fund. Likewise, require him to disclose complete information required under PPSAS in its Notes to the Consolidation Financial Statements.

We recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 with regard to the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund for development projects to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommend that management stop charging to LDRRMF expenditures that are not related to disaster risk management activities and ensure that the MDRRM Plan reconcile with the relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and COA Circular 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 to avoid unnecessary charges to the LDRRMF. Further, cause the immediate submission of the Certificate of Title for the purchase of the land.

We recommend that management secure approval from the DSWD Secretary for the modification in the distribution of ESA from P30,000.00 to P10,000.00 per household, and submit an explanation on the exclusion of at least 348 persons as ESA beneficiaries even if they were previously included in the original list of ESA recipients.

We recommend that management strictly ensure that transactions involving fund transfers be supported by the required documents before such funds are released pursuant to the requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommend that the Social Welfare Development Officer be required to formulate policies and guidelines on the grant of financial assistance to individuals in crises situation and submit the same to the Sanggunian for approval pursuant to Section 483(b) of RA 7160.

We recommend that management formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations and submit the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the audit team; copy furnished the DBM, the House Committee on Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance, as mandated by Section 93 of RA 10651.

We recommend that 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized to help
achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes as
provided for in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated
April 13, 2011. Likewise, require the Bids and Award Committee to submit
procurement contracts and all the documents forming part thereof, for
evaluation within five (5) working days from its execution pursuant to Section
3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 22, 2009.

We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer be required to
maintain a separate registries and subsidiary records for the LDRRMF as
required in COA Circular No. 2012-002. Likewise, require the Municipal
Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for CY 2016 to
a Special Trust Fund and to disclose information pertaining to the LDRRM in
the Notes to Financial Statements as required in COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommend that the Local School Board include as topmost priority
the improvement of educational facilities and other priority
programs/projects/activities enumerated in Section 100 © of RA 7160 and DECS-
DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 Series of 1998. Likewise,
maximize its implementation to ensure that the desired benefits are obtained by a
greater number of intended beneficiaries.

We recommend that Revenue Collection Clerks be required to turn over
the full amount of their collections at the close of business day to the Municipal
Treasurer, and for the latter to deposit intact his collections, as well as collections
turned over to him by the collectors/ tellers, with the authorized depository bank
daily or not later than the next banking day, in accordance with Section 307 of
RA 7160 and Section 26 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3,1992.

We recommend that the management observe strictly the 55% Personnel
Services limitation in the preparation of the annual budget as required in
Section 325 (a) of RA 7160. The Municipal Mayor being the Local Chief
Executive should always be guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and
supplemental budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements were
completely complied with.

We recommend that the procuring office furnish the Auditor with the
required copies of contract documents/purchase/letter/job orders and their
supporting documents as well as notice of deliveries of procured items within the
period prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to submit the
accounts and financial reports and to strictly observe the timely submission of the
same in accordance with Section 347 of Republic Act 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of
COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39, PD 1445.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the result of post audit of the
accounts and financial reports.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant
be required to submit immediately the original copies of cancelled checks as
required by regulations.

We recommend that the management formulate an action plan to
implement the audit recommendations and submit immediately the duly
accomplished AAPSI form to the audit team; copy furnished the DBM, the
House Committee on Appropriation and the Senate Committee on Finance, as
mandated by Section 96 of RA 10717.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to immediately refund the amount of P14,740,000.0 and deposit the same to the LGU servicing bank. Likewise, strictly observe the rules and regulations in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as provided for in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: Deposited the amount of P 14,740,000.0 the remaining balance of cash advance. Likewise, strictly observe the rules and regulations in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as provided for in COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer deposited the amount; the remaining balance was refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe the minimum take home pay requirement of P3,000.00 for each employees pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 10651 so as not to affect the efficiency and morale of the employees.
observations.reason: There are still employees receiving less than the minimum take home pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management develop an updated annual LDRRMFIP to include the unexpected amounts of the prior years and to accrue any unexpected balance to a Special Trust Fund for inclusion in the financial statements and disclosed in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Deposit the unexpended LDRRMF to Trust Account.
observations.reason: The Municipal management deposited the LDRRMF to Trust Account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management ensure substantial compliance with Section 17.6 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. In no case shall infrastructure projects be implemented unless the detailed engineering for the projects have been sufficiently carried out and duly approved.
observations.managementAction: Solid Waste Management Plan attached to the voucher.
observations.reason: Solid Waste Management Plan has been formulated and attached.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management ensure substantial compliance with Section 17.6 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. In no case shall infrastructure projects be implemented unless the detailed engineering for the projects have been sufficiently carried out and duly approved.
observations.managementAction: Work attached to the voucher and lacking documents for detailed engineering complied.
observations.reason: Documents for detailed engineering already complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that fees collected for bid/procurement process be provided to suppliers who participated in the procurement process and refund the said honorarium pursuant to the provisions of Section 3.0 of DBM Budget Circular 2007-3 issued on November 29, 2007, under Appendix 23 of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Concerned BAC and TWG members informed and agreed to refund/returned the said honorarium received.
observations.reason: Refund/recommendation has already been complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management ensure substantial compliance with Section 21.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order to ensure the widest possible dissemination of the invitation to bid to interested parties/bidders in all procurement transactions of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Invitation to bid posted at PHILGEPS website, accidentally detached from the voucher. Copy of ITB posted during the exit conference.
observations.reason: Invitation posted; copy provided during exit conference.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant strictly observe the timely submission of accounts and financial reports in accordance with Section 347 of Republic Act 7160, Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and Section 39, PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The necessary reports & documents were submitted to the Accounting Office. The MTO submitted the reports to the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Lack of knowledgeable personnel in the Accounting Section & the necessary reports & documents were subsequently submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections on hand to their authorized depository bank and henceforth to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 307 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992, and Sections 29 & 49 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volumes I & II, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The reports of collection as of Dec. 31, 2013 were already deposited. The collections as of Dec. 31, 2016 were yet to be validated in an on-going cash collection examination by the audit team.
observations.reason: Some collections were deposited and report submitted; other collections are under audit validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should direct all concerned officials/employees to immediately unliquidate their outstanding cash advance. We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials and employees strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004.
observations.managementAction: Notified the concerned officials & employees regarding their liquidation reports. Some of the concerned officials & employees did not submit their liquidation reports.
observations.reason: Some officials/employees submitted liquidation reports; others have not. Recommendations are without prejudice to Notices of Suspension/Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and all other concerned to strictly implement the provisions of Sections 119 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I. Require the creation of an inventory team to conduct physical count of all PPE and prepare the subsidiary ledger.
observations.managementAction: Management already created an inventory team and conducted physical count.
observations.reason: Physical inventory count and subsidiary ledger were prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere with the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations, Executive Order No. 49 dated January 21, 1993 and related procurement guidelines. This recommendation is without prejudice to issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance resulting from post-audit of transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with procurement recommendations and purchased medicines through proper process.
observations.reason: Procurement process later complied with; purchases done accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the guidelines under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and deposit the unexpended LDRRMF to Trust Account and submit a report on its utilization.
observations.reason: LDRRMF was deposited to Trust Account; recommendation without prejudice to Notices of Suspension/Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly implement the provisions of Section 4 (6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 requiring contracts and verified accomplishment reports prior to payment.
observations.managementAction: Accomplishment Reports were already prepared prior to payment and given to the Job Order status employees.
observations.reason: Accomplishment reports and contracts were later prepared and payments substantiated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive exercise command responsibility in ensuring that financial accounts, reports and records be submitted for audit within the prescribed time frames, pursuant to the provisions of RA 7160 and COA Circular 95-006.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: The missing disbursement vouchers were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 74 of PD 1445, Section 3.2 of COA Circular 96-001 and Section 10, Chapter 1 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs Vol. II and to prepare the necessary bank reconciliations.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant complied with bank reconciliation statements from September to December 2013.
observations.reason: Municipal Accountant prepared reconciliations for certain months; full reconciliation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the subsidiary ledgers and to exert extra efforts in identifying particulars of unliquidated cash advances and to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record necessary adjustments to come up with correct account balances.
observations.managementAction: Prepared the necessary adjustments and journal entry voucher to offset the adjustments.
observations.reason: Some adjustments prepared; further work ongoing and recommendations without prejudice to Notices of Suspension/Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere strictly with the provisions of Section 325 (a) of RA 7160 and Local Budget Circular No. 98 and consider the PS limitation in preparation of the annual budget.
observations.managementAction: PS Limitation has been complied in FY 2016. Management has been paying terminal leave benefits to retired/separated from the service LGU employees. The LGU promised to take into consideration the limit in the preparation of the budget.
observations.reason: PS limitation complied in FY 2016; excess due to terminal leave payments which were accounted for.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly implement the provisions of Section 27 of the General Appropriations Act and prepare a GAD Plan and provide budget for its activities, monitor and report on the same.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan has been prepared and implemented every year. GAD focal point chairperson designated.
observations.reason: GAD Plan prepared and implemented annually.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned individuals be required to refund the amounts received for cash gifts and henceforth stop the granting of cash gift to employees who are engaged through job order.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped granting cash gifts to employees engaged through JO. Those who received were already required to refund.
observations.reason: Cash gifts discontinued and refunds obtained.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant submit the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents and strictly observe the timely submission of accounts as required by regulations.
observations.managementAction: Some disbursement vouchers were submitted.
observations.reason: Some vouchers remain outstanding; others were submitted; matter subject to post-audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the 38 disbursement vouchers totaling P1,035,996.66, otherwise this office will disallow the subject disbursements and impose sanctions provided under Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code. Also require the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to submit transactions/reports on time.
observations.managementAction: Some of the disbursement vouchers were submitted.
observations.reason: Some vouchers submitted; effort to submit remaining transactions ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare an inventory list of all insurable properties for insurance coverage and conduct physical count of PPE. Properties that are unserviceable be dropped from the books. Apply for insurance coverage administered by GSIS.
observations.managementAction: Motor vehicles were insured at the General Fund administered by GSIS. Buildings and other equipment were not yet insured.
observations.reason: Motor vehicles insured; other properties pending insurance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Government provide budget for GAD activities and implement and monitor the program.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan prepared and budget provided for activities every year.
observations.reason: GAD Plan and budget now provided annually.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant establish the breakdown of the balances of the accounts, prepare subsidiary ledgers and index of payments, and provide extra effort to prepare reconciliation of deductions and remittances to avoid accumulation of unreconciled and unremitted amounts. The Municipal Treasurer should remit withholding taxes and other trust liabilities pursuant to prescribed regulations.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant established the breakdown of the accounts and prepared the reconciliation statements.
observations.reason: Breakdown and reconciliations were prepared; remittances being monitored.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor discontinue the hiring of consultants whose functions are mere duplication of those already in the service; fill up vacant positions instead of hiring consultants where necessary; and strictly comply with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2004-130 and Section 3.2 of COA Audit Circular No. 94-001.
observations.managementAction: Hiring of consultants stopped and vacant positions filled where necessary.
observations.reason: Consultant hiring stopped; vacant positions filled and provisions complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Development Plan be formulated to include time frames for programs and projects to facilitate monitoring and implementation. Require that copies of perfected contracts and supporting documents be submitted within five (5) days after approval and that program of works, specifications and detailed estimates be prepared by responsible officials.
observations.managementAction: Program of works and specifications were partially prepared; copies of perfected contracts were partially submitted.
observations.reason: Some works, specifications and estimates were prepared; others are pending; copies of contracts to be submitted within five (5) days.
